name: Planet Labs PBC

profile.description: Planet Labs operates the world's largest constellation of Earth-imaging satellites, providing daily global imaging data and AI-powered analytics for defense, agriculture, climate monitoring, and commercial applications. The company achieved first-ever annual positive adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow in fiscal year 2026.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Planet Labs delivered record FY2026 revenue of $307.7M (+26% YoY), with Q4 at $86.8M (+41% YoY), and achieved first-ever annual positive adjusted EBITDA ($15.5M) and free cash flow ($52.9M) [1]. Defense & Intelligence revenue grew 50%+ YoY. AI Deployment (2/2): Partnerships with Google and NVIDIA for AI-powered satellite data processing; petabyte-scale archive serves as 'ground truth' for physical AI applications; Tanager satellites provide independent methane and carbon emissions verification [1]. Market Share (2/2): Operates the world's largest commercial Earth-imaging satellite constellation with daily global coverage; secured $240M Germany and 9-figure Sweden Satellite-as-a-Service deals, establishing sovereign data leadership [1]. Pipeline (1/2): $900M+ backlog (+79% YoY) with 116% net dollar retention rate provides strong revenue visibility; Satellite-as-a-Service model expanding to sovereign customers [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Continuous satellite constellation innovation with miniaturized, cost-effective satellites launched at industry-leading cadence.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Planet's mission to image the entire Earth daily and make change visible and actionable serves clear environmental and security purposes — enabling climate monitoring, deforestation tracking, and disaster response [1]. As a Public Benefit Corporation, the mission is legally embedded. Community Impact (2/2): Satellite data supports climate disclosure mandates, agricultural optimization, and disaster response globally. Partnerships with governments for sovereign Earth observation directly serve national security. Employee Retention (1/2): Attracts passionate talent at the intersection of space, data science, and environmental impact. CEO Will Marshall's vision inspires mission-driven employees. Insider Buying (1/2): Management has delivered on financial milestones including first positive EBITDA and FCF, demonstrating alignment with long-term value creation [1].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Planet Labs is transitioning from loss-making to profitable. Revenue grew 41% in Q4 FY2026 to $86.8M with 59% non-GAAP gross margin [1]. First annual positive adjusted EBITDA ($15.5M) and FCF ($52.9M) are milestone achievements. However, D/E of 245.4% is high. ROE of -78.4% remains deeply negative on a GAAP basis. Profit margin of -80.2% reflects GAAP losses from stock compensation and depreciation. Forward P/E is deeply negative (-4070x). The $11.5B market cap is heavily dependent on growth expectations. Liquidity floor: 12.8M avg vol x $33.82 = $433M — passes [2].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Planet Labs is an AI enabler, not a target. Its petabyte-scale Earth imaging archive serves as irreplaceable 'ground truth' training data for AI models, creating partnerships with Google and NVIDIA [1]. The company's unique data asset — daily global satellite imagery — cannot be replicated by AI; rather, AI makes the data more valuable through automated analysis, change detection, and predictive analytics. Climate disclosure mandates requiring verified emissions data further entrench Planet's position. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI-powered analytics transform Planet from a data company to an insights company, enabling higher-value products and services on top of the imaging data [1].

infinite_game.summary: Planet Labs as a Public Benefit Corporation is structurally committed to long-term thinking, with a mission to make global change visible and actionable. The company's patient 12-year journey from startup to profitability, while continuously expanding its satellite constellation, exemplifies infinite game principles.

just_cause.analysis: Planet's mission to image the entire Earth every day and make change visible, accessible, and actionable is among the most compelling just causes in technology [1]. As a PBC, this purpose is legally embedded in the corporate charter. The data enables climate monitoring, agricultural optimization, disaster response, and national security — serving causes larger than any single product cycle.

trusting_teams.analysis: CEO Will Marshall has built a culture of mission-driven engineers and scientists. The company's consistent execution — reaching profitability milestones while expanding the satellite constellation — reflects functional team dynamics. The 116% net dollar retention rate suggests customers (and the teams serving them) are performing well [1].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Planet explicitly acknowledges and learns from rivals including Maxar (now part of MDA), Airbus Defence & Space, and Capella Space, using their high-resolution capabilities to inform its own product strategy while differentiating through daily global coverage and AI analytics [1].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Planet has evolved from an imagery company to a data-as-a-service platform, and now to a Satellite-as-a-Service provider (Germany $240M, Sweden deals) [1]. This progression shows flexibility in business model while maintaining the core mission of Earth observation.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Maintaining the PBC structure as a public company, investing through years of losses to build the world's largest commercial satellite constellation, and pivoting to sovereign Satellite-as-a-Service demonstrate conviction. Sharing data openly for climate monitoring shows purpose beyond profit [1].

investment_thesis: Planet Labs has crossed the profitability inflection — first annual positive adjusted EBITDA ($15.5M) and FCF ($52.9M) validate the business model after years of investment [1]. Revenue of $307.7M (+26%) with $900M+ backlog (+79%) provides strong visibility to continued acceleration. The AI Renaissance creates a structural tailwind as Planet's petabyte-scale archive becomes 'ground truth' for physical AI, attracting Google and NVIDIA partnerships [1]. The Satellite-as-a-Service model (Germany $240M, Sweden 9-figure deal) opens a new revenue vector addressing sovereign demand for independent Earth observation capabilities. Defense & Intelligence revenue growing 50%+ represents a high-value, sticky government customer base.
thesis_continued: The bear case focuses on valuation and profitability sustainability. The $11.5B market cap on $307.7M revenue (37x sales) prices in significant growth acceleration that must materialize [2]. GAAP losses remain substantial with -80.2% profit margin, and the adjusted profitability is modest ($15.5M EBITDA on $307.7M revenue). Commercial sector revenue declined YoY as the company prioritized government customers, narrowing the revenue base [1]. NRO EOCL contract rumors (30% cuts) could materially impact government revenue. D/E of 245.4% is high for a company still in early profitability. The satellite hardware business requires ongoing constellation replacement spending that could pressure FCF sustainability. Competition from Maxar/MDA, Airbus, and emerging players intensifies as the Earth observation market grows [1].
key_catalysts: Backlog conversion — $900M+ backlog (+79% YoY) with 116% net dollar retention driving revenue acceleration into FY2027 [1]
Satellite-as-a-Service expansion — Germany ($240M) and Sweden (9-figure) sovereign deals creating a new high-value recurring revenue stream [1]
AI partnership monetization — Google and NVIDIA collaborations converting Planet's data asset into higher-value AI-powered analytics products [1]
key_risks: Valuation risk — $11.5B market cap at 37x revenue requires sustained 25%+ growth and margin expansion to justify [2]
Government contract concentration — Defense & Intelligence grew 50%+ but NRO EOCL budget risks could reduce the largest customer segment [1]
GAAP profitability gap — adjusted EBITDA is positive but GAAP losses of -80.2% margin create divergence between reported and operational reality [2]
sources: Planet Labs FY2026 Q4 Earnings — $307.7M annual revenue (+26%), $86.8M Q4 (+41%), first positive EBITDA/FCF, $900M+ backlog, March 19, 2026
Yahoo Finance / yfinance data for PL, retrieved March 23, 2026
